message:
fix(fcp-intercom): enforce Intercom endpoint host allow-list at configure boundary (resolves br-gs71m)

Resolves flywheel_connectors-gs71m — [REVIEW-A9][P1] connector
security: configure() previously accepted any base_url whose
shape was userinfo/query/fragment-clean, even if the host was not
an Intercom API endpoint.

**Previous behavior (auth-routing-to-attacker):**

`IntercomConfig::from_params` called `reject_base_url_qfu` to
refuse userinfo/query/fragment shapes (good — closes the
`https://api.intercom.io?leak=token` vector), but THEN accepted
any host. `https://intercom.example.com` passed both the
URL-shape check AND the substring diagnostic in
`provisioning_readiness`, so `IntercomClient` was constructed
with the bearer token pointing at the attacker host. The
existing `base_url_policy` function ran on the readiness path
only — informational, not enforcing.

**Fix (mirrors zapier 63493e6e / teams 9a2aabd2 / datadog c2339e04):**

- New `ALLOWED_INTERCOM_HOSTS` constant lists the three regional
  Intercom API endpoints per
  https://developers.intercom.com/docs/build-an-integration/learn-more/rest-apis/api-base-urls/:
  `api.intercom.io` (US default), `api.eu.intercom.io` (EU),
  `api.au.intercom.io` (AU). The pre-fix policy only allowed
  `api.intercom.io`, so EU/AU operators couldn't even configure
  with their regional host.
- `base_url_policy` now does exact-host equality against the
  allow-list (eq_ignore_ascii_case for case-folding). Substring
  matching would let `intercom.io.evil.example` and
  `evil-intercom.io` through; exact equality blocks both.
- `from_params` calls `base_url_policy` after `reject_base_url_qfu`
  and converts a non-OK result into a hard
  `FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1003 }`. The `IntercomClient`
  is never constructed for off-policy URLs.
- localhost / 127.0.0.1 / ::1 stay allowed for tests via the
  unchanged `is_local_test_host`.

**Test changes (3 inverted, 5 new, all passing):**

Inverted (were asserting buggy permissive behavior):
- `config_custom_base_url` → `config_custom_base_url_within_policy`:
  was asserting `https://intercom.example.com` accepted; now uses
  `https://api.eu.intercom.io`.
- `config_clone_preserves_base_url`: was using `https://custom.io`
  (now policy-rejected); switched to `https://api.au.intercom.io`.
- `provisioning_readiness_custom_base_url_rejected` →
  `from_params_rejects_non_intercom_host`: rejection now happens
  at from_params construction, not at the readiness view; updated
  assertion target.

New regressions:
- `from_params_rejects_substring_collision_host`: locks in
  exact-host equality vs. substring matching.
- `from_params_rejects_http_non_local_host`: https-required gate.
- `from_params_accepts_all_regional_hosts`: regression guard
  ensuring all three regional API hosts pass the policy
  (US/EU/AU).
- `from_params_accepts_localhost_for_tests`: localhost / 127.0.0.1
  test allowance preserved.

235 fcp-intercom tests passing (206 lib + 29 integration). Same
defense pattern as br-898o4 (zapier), br-ieiby (datadog), and the
teams fix: validate at construction, not surface violations as
informational status fields callers may ignore.
